name: Sendinรชs (Mirandese meridional)
notes: Sendinรชs subdialect of Mirandese, spoken in Vila de Sendim (southern extreme of Terra de Miranda). Differs from central Mirandese in: (1) monophthongisation of โจiรช/ieโฉ โ /i/ and โจuรด/uoโฉ โ /u/ (tirra not tiรชrra, curpo not cuรดrpo); (2) NO word-initial /ส/ palatalisation: initial โจl-โฉ stays /l/ (luna not lhuna, lobo not lhobu). The Primeiro Aditamento ร  Convenรงรฃo (February 2000) explicitly permits Sendinese writers to use โจl-โฉ word-initially to represent /l/ (not /ส/). Sendinese uses โจieโฉ/โจuoโฉ (no accent marks) where central Mirandese writes โจiรชโฉ/โจuรดโฉ, reflecting absence of the diphthong.

stress.notes: Convenรงรฃo Ortogrรกfica da Lรญngua Mirandesa (1999) accentuation, Portuguese-based: paroxytone default; written accent (acute or tilde) overrides. Oxytone-attracting endings reflect Mirandese orthography: consonant-final r/l/z/รง (rapaรง, Pertual, falar) and the final nasals written with -n in the Asturleonese manner โ -in (camin, Sendin), -un, and the systematically oxytone -on class from Latin -ลnem (naรงon, coraรงon, Associaรงon). Unmarked -an/-en verb endings stay paroxytone as in Portuguese -am/-em. Dialect mwl-x-sendim: no evidence of stress-pattern divergence from standard Mirandese; block copied from mwl.
